name: South Korea
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **KOSPI 7,498.00 (+0.1% 1D, +36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M, +78% YTD) — LARGEST 30-DAY MOVE IN ANY MAJOR-MARKET INDEX IN RECENT MEMORY. SAMSUNG +111% YTD, SK HYNIX +144% YTD; SAMSUNG $1T MARKET CAP HOLDING.** With Brent at $101 and Hormuz blocked Day 71, the AI cycle is producing the largest 30-day rally in major-market history. Markets making binary call: AI capex super-cycle DOMINATES Iran-war risk premium. **CONTRADICTION SIGNAL:** USD/KRW ₩1,461.48 (+1.2% 1D) — won WEAKENING despite KOSPI strength. Suggests foreign capital flowing in (KOSPI bid) while broader macro (DXY) and Korean trade data are softer. The won-weakness amid equity strength is the cleanest single tell that the KOSPI gain is now FOREIGN-flow-led not domestic-fundamentals. **Helium-cascade thesis EVOLVED FROM 'FALSIFIED' TO 'BUFFER CLOCK ACTIVE':** Korean fab buffer 6-12 weeks per Smith/Discovery Alert puts absorptive capacity through ~mid-June (~5-6 weeks remaining as of May 9). Cheniere CCO May 7: 'astounded that gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month' — markets pricing-through. Samsung Q1 + SK Hynix sufficient-inventory framings still hold; SK hynix says 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided). **STRUCTURAL ASSESSMENT (THE MOST REVERSIBLE POSITION IN THE WORLD):** if Hormuz remains closed past Korean fab buffers depleting (~mid-June), the KOSPI 36.5% MoM gain is the most reversible position in the world. The AI capex story is ~95%; the helium tail is ~5%; only the 5% reverses on ceasefire. Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks remain preliminary, no orders placed. For India deployment: Korea continues to outperform in AI-cycle bifurcation thesis.
